qa_pairs.question: In Ansys Fluent, how can Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injections be automated using a journal file, and what is the typical structure of a basic injection text file for this purpose?
qa_pairs.answer: To automate Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injections within Ansys Fluent using a journal file, you can include commands such as `/define/models/dpm/interaction/coupled-calculations yes`, `/define/models/dpm/injections/delete-injection injection-0:1`, and `/define/models/dpm/injections/create injection-0:1 no yes file no zinjection01.inj no no no no`. A basic injection text file, like `zinjection01.inj`, often follows a steady format with particle properties defined as: `(z=4 12) ( x y z u v w diameter t mass-flow mass frequency time name ) (( 2.90e-02 5.00e-03 0.0 -1.00e-03 0.0 0.0 1.00e-04 2.93e+02 1.00e-06 0.0 0.0 0.0 ) injection-0:1 )`.

qa_pairs.question: How can users view the command-line options for the `cfx5solve` executable in Ansys CFX, and what are the specific options for setting calculation precision and enabling support for large meshes?
qa_pairs.answer: To view all available command-line options for `cfx5solve` in Ansys CFX, users should first load the appropriate module version and then execute `cfx5solve -help` in the terminal. Key options include `-single` to run in single precision (which is the default), `-double` to run in double precision (which doubles memory requirements), and `-large` to enable support for meshes with up to 2 billion elements (compared to the default limits of 80 million structured or 200 million unstructured elements).

qa_pairs.question: When submitting Ansys CFX jobs to an Alliance cluster via Slurm, how are parallel runs configured for both single-node and multi-node scenarios, and what are the essential Slurm script commands for these configurations?
qa_pairs.answer: For single-node parallel execution of Ansys CFX on an Alliance cluster, a Slurm script should specify `--nodes=1` and use a command like `cfx5solve -def YOURFILE.def -start-method "Open MPI Local Parallel" -part $SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE` (for Narval or Fir clusters) or `"Intel MPI Local Parallel"` (for other clusters). For multi-node parallel execution, the script would specify `--nodes=2` (or more) and use `cfx5solve -def YOURFILE.def -start-method "Open MPI Distributed Parallel" -par-dist $NNODES` (for Narval or Fir) or `"Intel MPI Distributed Parallel"` (for other clusters), where `NNODES` is determined by `$(slurm_hl2hl.py --format ANSYS-CFX)`.
